title: Listening - Part 4: Life in Dance Companies
description: You will hear five short extracts in which professional dancers talk about their life in dance companies. You must complete two tasks while listening.

task1.title: Task One
task1.instruction: For questions 21 – 25, choose from the list (A – H) how each speaker feels before they go on stage.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 mentions that 'knowing that there’ll be reviews of my performance – that’s still enough to set me off'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 worries about the physical toll: 'I wonder what I’d do if I strained something so badly that it’d put me out of action'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 recalls a past embarrassment where their 'mind went blank' and says 'that still haunts me when I’m waiting in the wings'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 expresses anxiety about the team dynamic: 'I get flustered thinking that I’m part of a group and they rely on me'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 is 'neurotic about being stuck in front of an audience without something crucial to my role' and triple checks their attire.

options.A: excited by the prospect of a live audience
options.C: worried about forgetting what to do
options.E: frustrated by the lack of adequate rehearsal time
options.F: fearful of not having the right equipment
options.G: overwhelmed by the scale of the venue

task2.title: Task Two
task2.instruction: For questions 26 – 30, choose from the list (A – H) what each speaker appreciates about their career.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 admits, 'I do get a kick out of being recognised in the street'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 notes, 'I never thought I’d see so many unusual places' due to the company touring.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 says 'dancing to the works of such a wide range of composers enriches my life'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 enjoys 'pushing boundaries' and working with 'young choreographers who use experimental designs'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 runs workshops and says, 'I get such a buzz from being a role model' to young dancers.

options.D: the close friendships formed with fellow dancers
options.F: the exposure to a variety of music
options.G: the satisfaction of mastering difficult techniques
options.H: the chance to inspire young dancers
